  • P189 — From AGI to ASI [#133, Moderately Innovative]
  • P001 — A Self-Consistency-Based Reranking for Narrative Question Answering [#177, Incremental]

Hardware Acceleration & Numerical Computing — avg: 62 (Innovative)

This cluster spans specialized hardware, system-level optimization, and numerical algorithms for AI and scientific computing. P027 (SMEPilot) dynamically selects CPU-only, SME-only, or mixed execution per operator using multi-ceiling roofline modeling of Arm Scalable Matrix Extensions. P087 introduces massively parallel flow routing on digital elevation models via high-to-low numbering making diagonal blocks triangular. P095 (LEWIS-60) benchmarks LLM inference systems jointly on performance (TTFT, latency, throughput) and output integrity (correctness, completeness, explainability). P097 (Ghost) provides OS-level GPU virtualization enabling elastic sharing and strong isolation for co-located AI workloads via NVIDIA kernel driver integration. P103 (CORE) unifies CPU, GPU, memory frequency scaling for mobile LLM inference via single offline-profiled energy-aware governor. P113 proposes a five-stage pipeline and four-level rubric (CAAR) for systematically analyzing embodied benchmark construction. P116 (URNG) enables single graph index to support four interval-aware ANN query types via unified relative neighborhood graph structure. These papers bridge the gap between algorithmic innovation and hardware reality, addressing deployment constraints on real systems.
  • P097 — Breaking the Tradeoff: Elastic and Isolated GPU Sharing with Ghost [#22, Innovative]
  • P116 — Efficient Graph Indexing for Interval-Aware Vector Search [#23, Innovative]
  • P103 — Rethinking DVFS for Mobile LLMs: Unified Energy-Aware Scheduling with CORE [#60, Innovative]
  • P027 — SMEPilot: Characterizing and Optimizing LLM Inference with Scalable Matrix Extensions [#68, Innovative]
  • P087 — Massively parallel flow routing and drainage area determination [#75, Innovative]
  • P113 — Intelligent Automation for Embodied Benchmark Construction: Pipelines, Embodiments, Simulators, and Trends [#130, Moderately Innovative]
  • P095 — LEWIS-60: BENCHMARKING FOR JOINT CHARACTERIZATION OF ARTIFACT INTEGRITY AND PERFORMANCE IN AI-AS-A-SERVICE SYSTEMS [#163, Incremental]

Agent Self-Evolution & Value Alignment — avg: 61 (Innovative)

This cluster explores how agents autonomously improve, adapt, and maintain value alignment across multicultural contexts. P151 proposes LLM-as-Environment-Engineer: the current RL policy analyzes its own failures and redesigns the next training stage. P161 introduces value diversity as system-level metric for multicultural multi-agent systems, benchmarking 18 LLM backbones across 19 cultures via World Values Survey responses. P165 trains a VLM to generate harder, more visually-grounded questions from unlabeled images using only the model itself—no annotations. P166 (OPD-Evolver) trains a compact LLM to holistically manage all four experience-lifecycle stages (selection, execution, writing, maintenance) via on-policy self-distillation. P172 (VibeThinker-3B) achieves frontier reasoning in 3B parameters via multi-stage post-training (curriculum SFT → RL → offline distillation → instruction RL). P173 (Data2Story) chains seven agents to transform raw data into multimedia articles with AI-generated images, video, audio, and widgets—each claim traceable to source. P185 (HarnessX) formalizes agent harnesses as typed, composable first-class objects and evolves them from execution traces via four-stage AEGIS engine. P186 (APPO) shifts credit assignment from coarse tool-call boundaries to fine-grained token-level decisions via branching score. Together, these papers move from static agents toward continuously self-evolving systems that operate transparently within multicultural value systems.
  • P166 — OPD-Evolver: Cultivating Holistic Agent Evolver via On-Policy Distillation [#69, Innovative]
  • P172 — VibeThinker-3B: Exploring the Frontier of Verifiable Reasoning in Small Language Models [#81, Innovative]
  • P186 — APPO: Agentic Procedural Policy Optimization [#85, Innovative]
  • P151 — From Trainee to Trainer: LLM-Designed Training Environment for RL with Multi-Agent Reasoning [#89, Innovative]
  • P161 — Beyond Alignment: Value Diversity as a Collective Property in Multicultural Agent Systems [#90, Innovative]
  • P185 — HarnessX: A Composable, Adaptive, and Evolvable Agent Harness Foundry [#98, Innovative]
  • P173 — Data Journalist Agent: Transforming Data into Verifiable Multimodal Stories [#104, Moderately Innovative]
  • P165 — Self-Evolving Visual Questioner [#138, Moderately Innovative]

Time Series, Forecasting & Resource Management — avg: 60 (Innovative)

This cluster addresses prediction and management of temporal phenomena at scale. P007 introduces OLFTSA, an overlapping functional time series framework jointly forecasting five geomagnetic indices at 6–24 hour horizons using rolling FPCA and varimax rotation. P054 (ORCA) is a gradient-free adapter improving frozen Time Series Foundation Model predictions by learning residuals conditioned on input and output. P069 (CloudCons) evaluates whether time-series forecasting accuracy translates to better cloud resource consolidation decisions across multi-cloud workloads. P081 (PowerPhase) introduces a probabilistic forecasting benchmark for power transmission grids with up to 36,964 jointly forecasted channels, plus OrderedQuantile forecasting with scenario generation. P098 (TimeRouter) selects among frozen TSFMs using XGBoost one-vs-all classification over hand-crafted time-series statistics. The thread is pragmatic: these papers measure whether improved forecasting actually improves downstream decisions, and exploit foundation models across heterogeneous temporal domains.
  • P054 — Learning the Context of Errors: Black-Box Online Adaptation of Time Series Foundation Models [#59, Innovative]
  • P007 — Stable Multivariate Functional Time Series Prediction for Major Geomagnetic Indices [#95, Innovative]
  • P069 — CloudCons: A Comprehensive End-to-End Benchmark for Cloud Resource Consolidation [#96, Innovative]
  • P081 — Navigating the Safety-Fidelity Trade-off: Massive-Variate Time Series Forecasting for Power Systems via Probabilistic Scenarios [#103, Moderately Innovative]
  • P098 — TimeRouter: Efficient and Adaptive Routing of Time-Series Foundation Models [#124, Moderately Innovative]

Graph Algorithms, Partitioning & Learning — avg: 60 (Innovative)

This cluster addresses algorithmic and learning approaches to graph problems spanning partitioning, preconditioners, and anomaly detection. P024 introduces parRSB, a parallel Recursive Spectral Bisection partitioner using AMG-preconditioned Fiedler vector computation, demonstrated on Summit and Frontier. P025 integrates a pre-trained MLP into Mt-KaHyPar to predict per-edge cut likelihood, gating contractions to avoid high-quality-cut edges. P052 and P063 address numerical methods: P052 shows standard GMRES bounds fail for two-level Schwarz preconditioners on space-time discontinuous Galerkin systems, while P063 couples FROSch overlapping Schwarz with FEATFLOW's geometric multigrid as a scalable coarse solver. P073 proves tight fundamental limits on hypergraph edge partitioning (MVF lower bound), suggesting data-oblivious schemes may be near-optimal. P075 introduces Ramba, the first selective state-space model for relational learning on multi-table databases. P079 achieves zero-shot graph anomaly detection by aligning source features via SVD and applying spectral normalization + K-means. P082 analyzes temporal conductance Φ(G) as an intrinsic connectivity parameter, bounding voter model consensus time with Θ bounds. These papers unite deterministic optimization, machine learning, and foundational complexity—bridging symbolic and neural approaches to large-scale graph problems.
  • P082 — Temporal Conductance and Bounds on the Voter Model for Dynamic Networks [#7, Highly Innovative]
  • P073 — Fundamental Limits of Hypergraph Edge Partitioning under Independent Edge Sampling [#9, Highly Innovative]
  • P025 — Engineering Learned Heuristics to Improve Clustering for Multilevel Graph Partitioning [#16, Innovative]
  • P075 — Ramba: Selective State-Space Models for Relational Deep Learning [#20, Innovative]
  • P052 — Analysis of Two-Level Schwarz Preconditioners for Space-Time Discontinuous Galerkin Method [#129, Moderately Innovative]
  • P063 — Combining Domain Decomposition and Multigrid Methods: A scalable interface between FROSch and FEAT [#142, Moderately Innovative]
  • P024 — parRSB: Exascale Spectral Element Mesh Partitioning [#152, Moderately Innovative]
  • P079 — A Zero-shot Generalized Graph Anomaly Detection Framework via Node Reconstruction [#153, Moderately Innovative]

Information Retrieval & Document Processing — avg: 58 (Moderately Innovative)

This smaller cluster focuses on extracting structure and retrieving information from documents and complex data. P057 benchmarks table extraction from 86k scientific PDF pages across four datasets, including two new ones (Table-arXiv from auto-annotated LaTeX, Table-BRGM manually-annotated geological documents). P067 compares Playwright vs. Selenium for BDD end-to-end UI testing in a custom HRMS, measuring execution time and maintenance effort. P132 (RACT) learns probabilistic column-to-table membership distributions, then constrains candidate matching targets before fine-grained similarity comparison. P188 (V-RAGBench) constructs a 2,100-query benchmark over hour-scale egocentric videos, decoupling retrieval and generation evaluation, and proposes CARVE (VideoRAG with modality-granularity selection). These papers address the practical challenge of extracting structured information from unstructured or semi-structured sources—a critical bottleneck in knowledge management.
  • P057 — Benchmarking Table Extraction from Heterogeneous Scientific PDF Documents [#4, Highly Innovative]
  • P188 — Rethinking RAG in Long Videos: What to Retrieve and How to Use It? [#32, Innovative]
  • P132 — RACT: Retrieval Augmented Column-Table Learning and Prediction for Multi-Table Schema Matching [#55, Innovative]
  • P067 — Playwright vs Selenium in Human Resources Management System [#191, Routine]

Multimodal LLMs & Vision Foundation Models — avg: 57 (Moderately Innovative)

This cluster explores multimodal models that fuse vision, audio, and language to solve grounded reasoning tasks. P003 constructs AudioDER, a 191k-sample post-training dataset for audio-language models with deduplication and chain-of-thought annotation. P033 fine-tunes a small MLLM (Qwen2-VL-2B) as a pairwise similarity ranker for person re-identification across domains. P071 introduces M*, a universal serving framework representing multimodal architectures as composable dataflow graphs, enabling model-agnostic scheduling. P072 benchmarks foundation models on power-distribution defect detection via a six-dimensional evaluation (perception, reasoning, tool use, etc.). P083 combines an 18-tool sandbox with two-stage training (GPT-5 SFT synthesis and OR-GIGPO RL) to teach VLMs spatial reasoning. P123 compresses video semantics offline into per-video LoRA adapters (Neural Knowledge Representations), enabling zero-token inference. P124 adapts frozen MLLMs for text tasks by optimizing a single input image via PEFT (Art-based Reinforcement Training). P137 reformulates video reasoning as iterative spatio-temporal evidence accumulation with nested spatial tools. P146 introduces games designed to isolate multimodal LLMs' ability to reconstruct hidden state from interaction history. P152 jointly trains spatial VLMs with language-only reasoning (deduction) and detect-then-reason (geometry) paths. P153 formulates long-form video understanding as agentic Observation-Thought-Action cycles with persistent textual memory. P169 streams real-time visual input, dynamically deciding each second whether to speak, delegate, or stay silent. These papers advance multimodal understanding from static image tasks toward interactive, temporal, and spatially-grounded reasoning.
  • P071 — M*: A Modular, Extensible, Serving System for Multimodal Models [#37, Innovative]
  • P123 — From Content to Knowledge: Lightning Fast Long-Video Understanding with Neural Knowledge Representations [#79, Innovative]
  • P146 — Beyond the Current Observation: Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models in Controllable Non-Markov Games [#80, Innovative]
  • P033 — Multimodal LLM-Empowered Re-Ranking for Generalizable Person Re-Identification [#87, Innovative]
  • P153 — Native Active Perception as Reasoning for Omni-Modal Understanding [#88, Innovative]
  • P083 — Perceive, Interact, Reason: Building Tool-Augmented Visual Agents for Spatial Reasoning [#99, Innovative]
  • P137 — Spatial Tool-Use Elicits Reasoning for Spatial Intelligence [#100, Innovative]
  • P124 — Fine-tuning Multi-Modal LLMs with ART: Art-based Reinforcement Training [#107, Moderately Innovative]
  • P152 — Reinforcing Dual-Path Reasoning in Spatial Vision Language Models [#115, Moderately Innovative]
  • P169 — JoyAI-VL-Interaction: Real-Time Vision-Language Interaction Intelligence [#121, Moderately Innovative]
  • P003 — AudioDER: A Deduplication-Enhanced Reasoning Dataset for Post-Training Large Audio-Language Models [#150, Moderately Innovative]
  • P072 — Multi-Modal Agents for Power Distribution Defect Detection: An Evaluation of Foundation Models [#165, Incremental]

Healthcare, Medical AI & Risk Assessment — avg: 56 (Moderately Innovative)

This smaller cluster applies LLMs and structured learning to medical and clinical contexts. P005 (CogGuard) combines LLM-based structured profiling (cognitive graphs for education, chaos-injection profiling for operations) with contrastive-regularized edge models to predict anomalies. P040 introduces a ruRoBERTa-large NER pipeline to extract structured entities (requirements, components, operating conditions) from Russian-language technical documentation. P164 trains LLMs to generate dialectical reasoning over competing clinical outcomes, extracting calibrated risk scores without explicit supervision. P192 (MedMisBench) benchmarks LLM robustness when false medical context is provided, measuring whether models maintain correct judgment under misleading scenarios. These papers address the high-stakes intersection of LLM reasoning and medical knowledge, emphasizing structured extraction, formal reasoning, and epistemic resilience.
  • P192 — MedMisBench: Measuring Epistemic Resilience of LLMs Under Misleading Medical Context [#3, Highly Innovative]
  • P164 — TRIAGE: Dialectical Reasoning for Explainable Risk Prediction on Irregularly Sampled Medical Time Series with LLMs [#11, Highly Innovative]
  • P005 — CogGuard: Cognitive and Operational Profiling for Proactive Warning in Edge Intelligent Services [#143, Moderately Innovative]
  • P040 — Semantic Analysis of Technical Documentation: Systematic Review, Formal Task Definition, and Transformer-Based NER Implementation [#178, Incremental]

World Models & Interactive Systems — avg: 53 (Moderately Innovative)

This smaller cluster explores world models and interactive agents that maintain persistent state and memory across episodes. P163 (LectūraAgents) generates personalized lectures with synchronized handwriting and annotation via multi-agent orchestration. P174 (DreamX-World 1.0) is a 5B general-purpose interactive world model generating long-horizon video with precise camera control and scene revisiting up to 16 FPS. P177 (VisualClaw) self-evolves a personalized multimodal agent by cascading frame gates and skill-bank injection, cutting video API costs by ~98%. P178 (Qwen-RobotWorld) unifies embodied prediction across manipulation, autonomous driving, and navigation via language-conditioned video generation. P183 introduces MRAgent, replacing static memory retrieval with iterative LLM-driven active reconstruction over heterogeneous graphs (Cue–Tag–Content), reducing tokens and improving accuracy. Together, these papers move from offline world models toward interactive, personalized, and continuously-evolving systems capable of long-term agency.
  • P183 — Memory is Reconstructed, Not Retrieved: Graph Memory for LLM Agents [#30, Innovative]
  • P177 — VisualClaw: A Real-Time, Personalized Agent for the Physical World [#114, Moderately Innovative]
  • P174 — DreamX-World 1.0: A General-Purpose Interactive World Model [#123, Moderately Innovative]
  • P163 — LectūraAgents: A Multi-Agent Framework for Adaptive Personalized AI-Assisted Learning and Embodied Teaching [#146, Moderately Innovative]
  • P178 — Qwen-RobotWorld Technical Report: Unifying Embodied World Modeling through Language-Conditioned Video Generation [#156, Moderately Innovative]

Agent Frameworks & Orchestration — avg: 53 (Moderately Innovative)

This cluster addresses the infrastructure and coordination challenges of deploying autonomous AI agents. P009 proposes MCP servers as middleware for agent context management, while P076 conceptualizes a distributed ecosystem (IoAI) where heterogeneous agents discover and negotiate with one another. P022 and P023 measure agent reliability: P022 evaluates AutoML agents on process correctness (leakage, reproducibility, compliance), and P023 enforces operational budgets in multi-agent security response via Lagrangian constraints. P046 scales LLM reasoning on dynamic graphs via adaptive partitioning, and P094 reformulates inference optimization as tree search with a checks-and-balances orchestrator. P066 positions agentic LLMs as knowledge-extraction agents rather than pure generators, while P090 audits systems for latent prompt injection vulnerabilities through evolved test cases. P105 chains three specialized agents over RAG for business analytics, and P107 deploys a hierarchical zero-shot framework for building data queries. P184 orchestrates omnimodal sub-agents by decomposing tasks into dependency-aware DAGs. The unifying theme is formal structure and orchestration: moving from monolithic agents to layered, verifiable, and coordinated systems.
  • P022 — GRACE-DS: a Guarded Reward-guided Agent Correction Environment in Data Science [#2, Highly Innovative]
  • P094 — Arbor: Tree Search as a Cognition Layer for Autonomous Agents [#26, Innovative]
  • P046 — AdaSTORM: Scaling LLM Reasoning on Dynamic Graphs via Adaptive Spatio-Temporal Multi-Agent Collaboration [#36, Innovative]
  • P184 — Orchestra-o1: Omnimodal Agent Orchestration [#42, Innovative]
  • P090 — PI-Hunter: Automated Red-Teaming for Exposing and Localizing Prompt Injections [#48, Innovative]
  • P023 — Safety-Contract Graph Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Autonomous Network Security Response [#82, Innovative]
  • P066 — Breaking the Knowledge Acquisition Bottleneck for System Health Digital Twins [#120, Moderately Innovative]
  • P107 — A ZERO-SHOT MULTI-AGENT FRAMEWORK FOR HUMAN-BUILDING INTERACTION VIA PROGRAMMATIC REASONING [#164, Incremental]
  • P076 — The Internet of Agentic AI: Communication, Coordination, and Collective Intelligence at Scale [#172, Incremental]
  • P105 — AI-POWERED SALES AND MARKETING ANALYTICS PLATFORM USING MULTI-AGENT AI [#183, Routine]
  • P009 — Model Context Protocol Servers as Orchestration Layers for Generative AI Agents in Enterprise Software Delivery [#189, Routine]

Cybersecurity, Adversarial Robustness & Verification — avg: 52 (Moderately Innovative)

This cluster spans defensive and offensive security challenges in AI systems and critical infrastructure. P018 introduces AGENTCYBERRANGE, a benchmark of 110 web vulnerabilities across 15 applications and 8 enterprise-like cyber ranges, with CAGE toolchain for evaluating autonomous agent security. P047 embeds backdoors in continual-learning anomaly detectors using autoencoder-based triggers aligned with clean-trajectory regularization. P058 pairs local open-weight LLMs with formal verifiers (ESBMC) to synthesize loop invariants without cloud APIs. P074 chains VAE and adversarial DNNs to defend SAP cloud logs against FGSM and PGD attacks. P111 proposes signature-based DDoS detection by inspecting forged HTTP headers (user-agent, referrer, URI). P114 formalizes policy documents into SMT-LIB logic offline, verifying LLM-generated answers against formalized policies at inference time. P129 conceptualizes a Cybersecurity Digital Twin architecture for modeling threats across multi-ownership digital service chains (IT, OT). P130 introduces a five-plane reference architecture for runtime governance of production AI agents. P181 demonstrates that perturbing flow-matching velocity predictions can corrupt autoregressive world models without ground-truth futures. The arc progresses from isolated defense (anomaly detection, backdoor resistance) toward system-level governance and formal verification.
  • P058 — Neuro-Symbolic Software Verification: Hyper-charging Local Language Models with Symbolic Reasoning at Scale [#13, Highly Innovative]
  • P114 — A Neurosymbolic Approach to Natural Language Formalization and Verification [#31, Innovative]
  • P018 — AGENTCYBERRANGE: Benchmarking Frontier AI Systems in Realistic Cyber Ranges [#44, Innovative]
  • P181 — BadWorld: Adversarial Attacks on World Models [#101, Innovative]
  • P130 — A Five-Plane Reference Architecture for Runtime Governance of Production AI Agents [#109, Moderately Innovative]
  • P111 — Detecting application layer DDoS attack using an advanced signature detection algorithm [#141, Moderately Innovative]
  • P129 — A Cybersecurity Digital Twin Architecture for Modelling Threats in Interconnected Systems [#147, Moderately Innovative]
  • P047 — Continual Backdoor Training in IoT/CPS [#174, Incremental]
  • P074 — AI-Enhanced Adversarial Attacks Vs. AI Defenses Specifically Targeting SAP Cloud Environments [#187, Routine]

Autonomous & Coding Agents — avg: 49 (Moderately Innovative)

This group focuses on autonomous agents that operate in code, mathematics, and open-ended problem spaces. P037 introduces dialogue-driven coding agents evaluated on repository-scale tasks through multi-turn natural language. P038 surveys agentic AI architectures across 280+ papers, unifying six components (perception, role, memory, planning, action, learning) and infrastructure requirements. P049 proposes canonicalization of human code repositories before agent training, reducing frontier-model requirements by 30–500×. P053 combines natural-language-guided graph partitioning with constraint-preserving QAOA for multi-drone assignment. P093 taxonomizes research agents on an L1–L5 autonomy scale and compares 17 systems. P099 implements agentic SAST (vulnerability scanning) using open-source LLMs instead of rule engines. P106 applies Straussian Grounded Theory to engineer narratives, identifying persistent human roles in agent-aided development. P175 decouples fast repository exploration from main coding agents via a 4B–30B specialized subagent. P036 proposes a three-stage organizational framework (AI-assisted → AI-integrated → AI-driven) for transitioning toward agentic development. Together, these papers chart the emerging role of autonomous code agents, from single-shot tasks to long-horizon research and software engineering workflows.
  • P175 — FastContext: Training Efficient Repository Explorer for Coding Agents [#15, Innovative]
  • P037 — Dialogue-SWEBench: A Benchmark for Dialogue-Driven Coding Agents [#27, Innovative]
  • P049 — No Accidental Software: Agent-First Canonical Code for Human-Code Entropy Reduction and 30×–500× Lower Frontier-Model Requirements [#112, Moderately Innovative]
  • P106 — The Role of the Software Engineer in Developing Software Using Coding Agents [#140, Moderately Innovative]
  • P099 — Can Open-Source LLM Agents Replace Static Application Security Testing Tools? An Empirical Assessment [#148, Moderately Innovative]
  • P036 — AI-driven Software Development: A Pragmatic Path to Agentic Development Processes [#149, Moderately Innovative]
  • P053 — Optimality-Preserving Decomposition for Scalable QAOA in Natural-Language-Guided Multi-Drone Assignment [#158, Incremental]
  • P038 — A Holistic Review of Agentic AI Frameworks, Applications, and Research Trajectories [#162, Incremental]
  • P093 — From Copilots to Colleagues: A Survey of Autonomous Research Agents [#169, Incremental]

Knowledge Graphs & Ontologies — avg: 48 (Moderately Innovative)

These papers collectively demonstrate a field-wide shift from abstract knowledge representation toward practical, AI-integrated semantic structures. P041 surveys knowledge graphs' established role across five healthcare domains, while P062 similarly reviews neurosymbolic AI advances—both establishing symbolic reasoning's maturity. Domain-specific ontologies anchor applied work: P042 introduces TGC for e-learning gamification, P044 presents OCO for materials engineering, P088 proposes ADO for AI-integrated digital twins, and P117 formalizes S-STEMIO for STEM identity measurement, each tailoring semantic frameworks to their discipline. The papers increasingly marry these ontologies with modern LLMs and neural methods: P055 uses NeRD to distill logical rules into multimodal reasoning chains for medical imaging, P119 combines LLaMA-2 with TransR knowledge graphs for education recommendation, P065 grounds LLM reasoning on wireless network graphs via KG-RAG, and P078 replaces unstructured text summaries with structured graph-based memory in dialogue agents. P045 and P115 propose overarching architectural frameworks—TGEO for interpretable LLM execution and a four-layer ontology architecture for geospatial reasoning—while P118 and P121 demonstrate specialized applications using ontologies for crop phenotype classification and spatial location description generation. Collectively, these papers reveal ontologies and knowledge graphs evolving from isolated semantic projects toward essential infrastructure for interpretable, domain-grounded AI reasoning.
  • P044 — A Multi-Level Architecture for Reusable Materials Ontologies — The OntoCrafter Ceramics Ontology (OCO) as Reference Implementation [#70, Innovative]
  • P055 — NeRD: Neuro-Symbolic Rule Distillation for Efficient Ontology-Grounded Chain-of-Thought in Medical Image Diagnosis [#91, Innovative]
  • P062 — A survey of neurosymbolic artificial intelligence: foundations, advances, and future trajectories [#105, Moderately Innovative]
  • P078 — G-Long: Graph-Enhanced Memory Management for Efficient Long-Term Dialogue Agents [#111, Moderately Innovative]
  • P088 — Semantic modelling of AI-integrated digital twins for enhanced transparency [#119, Moderately Innovative]
  • P118 — Ontology-driven dual-channel relational graph convolutional network (DCR-GCN) for lettuce leaf phenotype classification [#127, Moderately Innovative]
  • P115 — Multisource Knowledge Graph Architecture for Air-Gapped AI Systems: Design Patterns and a Geospatial Reference Implementation [#131, Moderately Innovative]
  • P119 — Intelligent English Education Resource Recommendation via LLM–Knowledge Graph Integration and Ecological Niche Fitness Modeling [#136, Moderately Innovative]
  • P117 — Making sense of student STEM identity: an ontological approach [#137, Moderately Innovative]
  • P042 — Towards Shareable Gamification Knowledge: An Ontological Approach to Support Teachers in Gamified E-Learning Systems [#144, Moderately Innovative]
  • P121 — Ontology-based Textual Location Description Framework for Multidimensional Features [#155, Moderately Innovative]
  • P041 — Semantic Reasoning in Medicine: The Role of Knowledge Graphs Across Five Key Domains [#170, Incremental]
  • P065 — Constructing Multimodal Wireless Knowledge Graphs for Large Language Model–Based Network Reasoning [#179, Incremental]
  • P045 — Theorem-Grounded Execution Ontologies for Interpretable Machine Reasoning [#182, Incremental]

Model Architecture, Reasoning & Synthesis — avg: 47 (Moderately Innovative)

This smaller but diverse cluster spans architectural innovations, long-horizon decision-making, and multimodal synthesis. P043 proposes Neuro-Symbolic Cognition Engines, a two-layer hybrid pairing neural Subsymbolic Perception with symbolic Symbolic Reasoning via bidirectional feedback. P092 surveys 280+ papers on long-horizon sequential decision-making across task domains and methodologies, offering failure taxonomy and LLM scaling laws. P167 (TV-Edit) extends instruction-based image editing with Content-Aware Spatial Controller to jointly accept text and sparse visual prompts, plus 23K training dataset. P170 shows efficient attention modules (sliding-window, recurrent mixers) in hybrid LLMs act as optimization priors, shaping how full-attention layers develop long-range retrieval heads. These papers bridge symbolic reasoning, architectural design, long-term planning, and multimodal control—uniting different facets of advancing AI reasoning and synthesis capabilities.
  • P170 — Rethinking the Role of Efficient Attention in Hybrid Architectures [#12, Highly Innovative]
  • P167 — Text-Vision Co-Instructed Image Editing [#76, Innovative]
  • P092 — Navigating the Long Horizon: A Comprehensive Survey of Agent Architectures and Reinforcement Learning for Extended Sequential Decision-Making [#161, Incremental]
  • P043 — Neuro-Symbolic Cognition Engines: Integrating Subsymbolic Perception with Symbolic Reasoning in Post-Deep-Learning AI [#192, Routine]

Education, Learning & Institutional Development — avg: 44 (Moderately Innovative)

This cluster explores AI and technology's role in education, skill development, and institutional transformation. P032 reports an 8-week controlled experiment on VR/AR/360° technologies for situational Business English speaking in Ukrainian universities. P059 applies empirical-Bayes shrinkage via Kalman smoothing to correct per-item bias in frozen knowledge-tracing models, recovering AUC headroom. P060 introduces RoboLang, a simplified custom programming language in a Unity game, measuring how grammar design influences novice learners' cognitive load and concept acquisition. P064 applies BERTopic to 514 LinkedIn posts to identify 22 topics characterizing how practitioners frame AI-enabled process mining. P127 surveys entrepreneurship education across 353 Pakistani students, showing that knowledge, skills, and experiential learning jointly explain 80% of entrepreneurial intention variance. P128 proposes the 'learning utility'—a three-layer institutional stack and 'GDP of learning' metric to convert data and AI into continuous bidirectional measurement of learning-system performance. Together, these papers address the transformation of education and institutions through technology, from classroom tools to organizational learning structures.
  • P059 — Recovering Stranded Discrimination in Knowledge Tracing: Per-Item Bias Correction via Empirical-Bayes Shrinkage [#1, Highly Innovative]
  • P128 — The bottleneck was never data or algorithms: building a learning utility for AI-enabled learning health systems [#118, Moderately Innovative]
  • P060 — Analyzing Novice Programming Behavior in Educational Game Environment: A Custom Language Study with Semantic Adaptation [#157, Moderately Innovative]
  • P064 — Emerging Narratives of AI-Enabled Process Mining: A Topic Modeling Analysis of Practitioner Discourse [#166, Incremental]
  • P032 — Application of Immersive Technologies in Teaching Business English in Higher Education Institutions for the Formation of Situational Speech [#171, Incremental]
  • P127 — The Role of Entrepreneurship Education in Cultivating Entrepreneurial Intentions Among Students: Evidence from the University Context [#185, Routine]

Enterprise, Business, Industrial & Defense Systems — avg: 38 (Incremental)

This large cluster spans organizational and sectoral applications of AI and digital transformation. P016 analyzes the Green-Collar Matrix, showing environmental crime, corruption, and money laundering are structurally interdependent in India's real estate sector. P017 (Posterior Twins) represents customer behavior as updated distributions under decision contexts, benchmarking TL-Twin variants on enterprise decisions. P034 applies action research to BPM program implementation in a Brazilian university's diploma issuance process. P039 combines six anomaly detectors with three-layer SHAP explainability for ERP procurement anomalies. P080 tests moderated mediation: does circular economy adoption mediate strategic entrepreneurship's effect on triple-bottom-line performance? P084 proposes five strategic directions for digital transformation of the global military-industrial complex. P086 qualitatively examines how technical debt manifests and is managed in enterprise cloud IT systems. P089 surveys Ukraine's MIC digitalization during 2020–2025 and proposes a prioritized roadmap. P108 chains rule-based controls, Isolation Forest, XGBoost, and Petri-net process mining for real-time SAP fraud detection. P109 (ADAPT) introduces a five-stage roadmap (Assess, Design, Assemble, Pilot, Transform) for deploying LLM agents in regulated financial institutions. P110 reports that self-reported GenAI-aided advertising positively predicts market performance among Nairobi food/beverage firms. P126 combines Hyperledger Fabric for drug supply-chain traceability with TRPO-based sentiment analysis and SHAP-guided feature selection. These papers collectively chart how organizations operationalize AI, from risk management and process improvement through strategic transformation.
  • P039 — Explainable Ensemble Learning Using SHAP for ERP Anomaly Detection [#50, Innovative]
  • P016 — The Green-Collar Matrix: An Empirical Analysis of the Tripartite Nexus among Environmental Crime, Corruption and Money Laundering in India's Real Estate Sector [#110, Moderately Innovative]
  • P126 — An intelligent drug supply chain management and recommendation framework using blockchain and TRPO-driven multi-agent learning [#126, Moderately Innovative]
  • P034 — Strategic convergence of Business Process Management and digital information technologies: action research of implementing BPM in a Brazilian public university [#154, Moderately Innovative]
  • P108 — SAP S/4HANA-Based Continuous Auditing and Fraud Detection in Integrated Accounting Systems [#160, Incremental]
  • P086 — Technical Debt in Enterprise Cloud IT Systems — A Qualitative Study on Types, Drivers, Impacts, and Mitigation [#167, Incremental]
  • P109 — Cognitive Agent Deployment Framework for Autonomous Financial Systems: A Multi-Stage Implementation Roadmap [#168, Incremental]
  • P080 — Strategic entrepreneurship circular economy practices and sustainable work performance among SMEs through institutional pathways for decent work and green economic growth [#173, Incremental]
  • P017 — Posterior Twins: Distributional Behavioral Simulation for Enterprise Decisions [#175, Incremental]
  • P110 — Generative AI-Aided Advertising as a Strategic Resource for Market Performance in Kenya's Food and Beverage Manufacturing Sector [#176, Incremental]
  • P089 — Digital Technologies as Key Vectors of Modernization of Ukraine's Military-Industrial Potential [#181, Incremental]
  • P084 — Strategic Guidelines for the Digital Transformation of the Military-Industrial Complex Worldwide [#188, Routine]

Manufacturing, Supply Chain & Industrial AI — avg: 34 (Incremental)

This cluster applies AI to optimize and understand industrial and manufacturing systems. P002 introduces XRDiff, a diffusion model recovering full 3D crystal structures from 1D powder X-ray diffraction patterns—bridging materials science and generative modeling. P019 synthesizes fault-tolerant data analytics pipeline architectures for IIoT-enabled smart manufacturing, covering ingestion through governance. P035 tests whether prescriptive analytics capability (PAC) mediates AI's impact on textile supply chain performance in Pakistan, quantifying path coefficients across supply, integration, and prediction. P056 surveys AI/ML/DL applications to industrial waste reduction, organized by waste classification, logistics, and circular economy. P061 formalizes physical and digital manufacturing entities as 5-tuple ontology components, automating M2M transformation to executable ROS 2 code. The unifying thread is systematic integration: moving from siloed manufacturing/materials tasks toward end-to-end data-driven pipelines and circular-economy thinking.
  • P002 — XRDiff: Crystal Structure Prediction from Powder X-Ray Diffraction Data Using Diffusion Models [#10, Highly Innovative]
  • P061 — Multi-Domain Fusion Meta-Model for Digital Twin in Precision Manufacturing: Design, Implementation and Verification [#151, Moderately Innovative]
  • P056 — Industrial Waste Reduction Applications of Artificial Intelligence: Progress, Challenges, and Future Directions [#180, Incremental]
  • P035 — Textile Supply Chain Optimization through Artificial Intelligence and Prescriptive Analytics Capability [#186, Routine]
  • P019 — Resilient Data Analytics Pipelines for Fault-Tolerant Smart Manufacturing Systems [#190, Routine]

Filtered Out (Non-Research)

The following documents were detected by the analyzer as non-research artifacts (workshop proposals, calls for participation, editorials, or announcements) and excluded from thematic grouping and innovation ranking.

  • [other] Materials Management Review, Volume 22 - Issue 8, June 2026 — This is a monthly trade magazine (Materials Management Review) published by the Indian Institute of Materials Management, containing a collection of practitioner articles, industry news, event reports, branch news, and award ceremony coverage — not a research paper reporting original findings, experiments, or formal contributions.
  • [announcement] Litio, una oportunidad histórica para un Chile Desarrollado — This is Chile's National Lithium Strategy — a government policy document announcing institutional creation plans, regulatory milestones, and public-private partnership frameworks; it contains no research findings, experiments, empirical analysis, or theoretical contributions.
  • [other] Multitask Learning for Membrane Permeability Prediction across Assays with Prediction Reliability Assessment — This PDF is the Supporting Information appendix for a JCIM research paper (DOI: 10.1021/acs.jcim.6c00597), containing only supplementary tables (S1–S9) and figures (S1–S6) with captions — no abstract, introduction, methods narrative, results discussion, or conclusions — and cannot be analyzed as a standalone research contribution.
  • [other] Instructor Manual: Chapter 15: Automated Incident Response and Orchestration — This is an instructor manual (teaching supplement) for a textbook chapter, containing lecture notes, discussion questions, laboratory exercises, assessment materials, and answer keys — it reports no research findings, experiments, or original technical contributions.
  • [other] Chapter 4 Instructor Manual: Machine Learning for Threat Detection — This is a course instructor manual (teaching guide) for a university chapter on ML-based cybersecurity, containing only learning objectives, weekly teaching schedules, lab exercise descriptions, assessment rubrics, and pedagogical troubleshooting advice — no research findings, experiments, theorems, or original contributions of any kind.
  • [title_mismatch] Machine Learning-Driven Combinatorial Optimization : A Systematic Review — PDF served by publisher was a different paper ('Improving Graph Matching with Positional Reconstruction Encoder-Decoder Network') — excluded from ranking.
  • [title_mismatch] Pages| Received 26 Aug. 2025, Accepted 20 May. 2026, Published online: 27 May. 2026 — PDF served by publisher was a different paper ('A survey on differential privacy methods for big data privacy protection') — excluded from ranking.
  • [title_mismatch] Personalisation and Article 22 GDPR — PDF served by publisher was a different paper ('Filter Bubbles, Fake News, and Algorithms: Perspectives on Media Diversity and P') — excluded from ranking.
